Fear and Greed is at
28
(Fear).
Bitcoin has been largely flat since yesterday's session,
consolidating just above
$63K
after last week's short-squeeze recovery from the $58K low.

The quiet price action masks a busy 24 hours underneath. A
governance attack just emptied a DAO treasury. Strategy sold
Bitcoin for the second time in weeks. And two separate firms kept
buying ETH and BTC into the weakness.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Strategy Just Sold Bitcoin Again to Pay Dividends

Strategy sold 3,588 BTC between June 29 and July 5, raising
approximately $216 million under a board-approved Bitcoin
Monetization Program. The sale came with BTC trading below the
company's average acquisition cost of $75,476.

The program authorises up to $1.25 billion in BTC sales for
dividends, reserve building, and share buybacks.

What are the three permitted uses of Strategy's Bitcoin
Monetization Program?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/DYroMtJtD3GuLQu7h4PHDw~~/AAQRxRA~/v7UbnjNkBJwKKnir5hQabxNitYEaNL19pM3c2Fhq6V71ykE2qstV_6pvHyNW5INrXgDpyejkl_jkugR3p0pzsHUhpgBT1R-niI2LPB3mB6cLZne0fi395VeUAqy48mUDpuOX7wD1NUExT7d3ETdECjxJNNI3dG8_KvTRm1uEdyA~ )
   BitMine Now Controls Nearly Five Percent of All Ethereum

BitMine purchased approximately $74 million in ETH during the
week ending June 28, bringing total holdings to 5.74 million ETH
worth nearly $10 billion.

Chairman Tom Le
